text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a software development practice where team members frequently merge their code changes into a shared main branch. Each integration is automatically verified by a CI pipeline. The pipeline first performs an automated build to compile the code and check for errors. It then runs a suite of tests, including unit and integration tests. This process is designed to catch issues early, before they become bigger problems. By doing so, CI helps developers identify and fix bugs quickly, improves code quality, and reduces the time it takes to release new features.
